name: Embalse de Arguis – circular trail running route
name_original: Embalse de Arguis – ruta circular de trail running
region_intro: Jogging routes around Arguis are set in a mountainous environment over 1000 meters above sea level, characterized by varied terrain and natural features. The region features the historic Arguis Reservoir, surrounded by pine forests and mountains like Gratal and El Águila. Part of the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park, the area offers diverse landscapes for running, including canyons formed by the Isuela River. Best jogging routes around Arguis The most popular jogging route is Embalse de Arguis – circular trail running route, a 3.4 miles (5.4 km) trail that takes 42 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic views of the reservoir and surrounding mountains. Another top favourite among local runners is Arguis Reservoir – Arguis Reservoir Dam loop from Arguis, a difficult 8.7 miles (14.0 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and panoramic views of the reservoir and the wider landscape. Local runners also love the Arguis Reservoir – Punta del Águila loop from Arguis, a 7.9 miles (12.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 6 minutes. Jogging around Arguis is defined by the Arguis Reservoir, mountainous terrain, and the diverse landscapes of the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging ascents. The routes in Arguis are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 900 runners have used komoot to explore Arguis's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Arguis?
A: Arguis offers over 30 dedicated running routes, catering to various skill levels. These trails are set in a mountainous environment over 1000 meters above sea level, providing diverse landscapes for your run.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes around Arguis?
A: The jogging routes around Arguis feature varied terrain, from accessible paths around the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2981345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; to more challenging ascents into the surrounding mountains like Gratal and El Águila. You'll encounter pine forests, open landscapes, and even canyons formed by the Isuela River, all within the stunning setting of the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate running routes suitable for beginners in Arguis?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for beginners. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950281532/embalse-de-arguis-circular-trail-running-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Arguis – circular trail running route&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 5.4 km long and offers scenic views of the reservoir on accessible paths. Another moderate choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5329644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir – Arguis Reservoir loop from Arguis&lt;/a&gt;, covering around 9.7 km.

Q: Can I find challenging trail running options in Arguis?
A: Absolutely. Arguis is known for its challenging mountainous terrain. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14942420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir – Arguis Reservoir Dam loop from Arguis&lt;/a&gt; (14 km with significant elevation gain) or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5329646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir – Punta del Águila loop from Arguis&lt;/a&gt; (12.8 km) offer demanding ascents and panoramic views, perfect for experienced trail runners. The area even hosts the 'Perimetrail de Arguis' mountain race, highlighting its suitability for advanced runners.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Arguis?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Arguis are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A prime example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950281532/embalse-de-arguis-circular-trail-running-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Arguis – circular trail running route&lt;/a&gt;, which loops around the Arguis Reservoir. Other circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14942420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir – Arguis Reservoir Dam loop from Arguis&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5329646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir – Punta del Águila loop from Arguis&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running in Arguis?
A: While running in Arguis, you can enjoy stunning natural features and landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2981345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a central highlight, offering beautiful water views. You might also encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5040942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgas de San Julián&lt;/a&gt; with its cascading water, or even the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5071759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;UFO Ridge&lt;/a&gt;. The routes also provide views of surrounding mountains like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349263" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de Gratal&lt;/a&gt; and Peña Roya.

Q: What do other runners say about their experience jogging in Arguis?
A: The running routes in Arguis are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 900 runners have used komoot to explore Arguis's varied terrain, often praising the scenic views of the reservoir and mountains, as well as the diverse challenges offered by the trails.

Q: Is Arguis a good destination for family-friendly jogging?
A: Arguis can be suitable for family-friendly jogging, especially around the Arguis Reservoir. The circular trail around the reservoir is generally accessible and offers a moderate run with beautiful scenery, making it enjoyable for families looking for a less strenuous outdoor activity. Always check the specific route details for difficulty and length to ensure it matches your family's fitness level.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in Arguis?
A: While the region is very natural and welcoming to outdoor activities, specific regulations regarding dogs on trails can vary, especially within the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. Always check local signage or park guidelines for the most current information.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Arguis?
A: Arguis, located over 1000 meters above sea level, offers a unique mountain environment. Spring and autumn are generally ideal for jogging, with pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer mornings and evenings can also be great, while winter might offer beautiful snowy runs, but requires appropriate gear and caution due to colder conditions and potential ice.

Q: Are there parking facilities available near the running trails in Arguis?
A: Yes, Arguis is conveniently accessible, and parking is generally available, especially near the Arguis Reservoir, which is a central starting point for many routes. The village itself also offers parking options. It's always a good idea to arrive early during peak times to secure a spot.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints along the running trails in Arguis?
A: Many running trails in Arguis offer spectacular viewpoints. Routes that ascend towards mountains like Pico Gratal or Punta del Águila provide panoramic vistas of the Sierra de Guara, the Huesca plain, and even the Pyrenees. The circular routes around the Arguis Reservoir also offer continuous scenic views of the water and surrounding peaks.
tips: This is the oldest reservoir in Aragon. It was under construction for 24 years and was completed in 1704. It dams the seasonal waters of the Isuela River and is used exclusively for irrigation (not counting, in addition, fishing), mainly from the Hoya de Huesca.

This picturesque town reminiscent of the Pyrenean villages for its stone houses topped by the typical espantabrujas of their chimneys. It also has a beautiful Romanesque-style church dating from the 12th century.
